root_causes.cause: R1 is dropping ICMP echo-replies from the 172.16.0.0/24 network because an inbound ACL (R1_IN) on R1 Serial0/1/0 denies ICMP sourced from 172.16.0.0/24.
evidence: R1 running-config: interface Serial0/1/0 has ip access-group R1_IN in (get_running_config:router1).
R1 running-config: access-list R1_IN sequence 10 = deny icmp 172.16.0.0 0.0.0.255 ... (shows ICMP deny for 172.16.0.0/24) (get_running_config:router1 access_lists).

OSPF adjacency is up between R1 and R2 on Serial0/1/0 (R1 neighbor 2.2.2.2 @ 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-full) — indicating routing/forwarding toward R2 is present and the interface is operational (get_ospf:router1).

root_causes.cause: R1 is dropping ICMP echo-replies from the 172.16.0.0/24 network because an inbound ACL (R1_IN) on R1 Serial0/1/0 denies ICMP sourced from 172.16.0.0/24.
evidence: R1 running-config: interface Serial0/1/0 has ip access-group R1_IN in (get_running_config:router1).
R1 running-config: access-list R1_IN sequence 10 = deny icmp 172.16.0.0 0.0.0.255 ... (shows ICMP deny for 172.16.0.0/24) (get_running_config:router1 access_lists).

OSPF adjacency is up between R1 and R2 on Serial0/1/0 (R1 neighbor 2.2.2.2 @ 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-full) — indicating routing/forwarding toward R2 is present and the interface is operational (get_ospf:router1).

data.intent_description: Investigate failed reachability: source 192.168.0.11 cannot reach destination 172.16.0.11 (25 pings sent, 0 successful). Goal: identify root cause and required remediation.

root_causes.cause: R1 is dropping ICMP echo-replies from the 172.16.0.0/24 network because an inbound ACL (R1_IN) on R1 Serial0/1/0 denies ICMP sourced from 172.16.0.0/24.
evidence: R1 running-config: interface Serial0/1/0 has ip access-group R1_IN in (get_running_config:router1).
R1 running-config: access-list R1_IN sequence 10 = deny icmp 172.16.0.0 0.0.0.255 ... (shows ICMP deny for 172.16.0.0/24) (get_running_config:router1 access_lists).

OSPF adjacency is up between R1 and R2 on Serial0/1/0 (R1 neighbor 2.2.2.2 @ 10.0.0.2 state ospf-nbr-full) — indicating routing/forwarding toward R2 is present and the interface is operational (get_ospf:router1).

data.intent_description: R1 is blocking ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24: inbound ACL R1_IN on router1 interface Serial0/1/0 denies ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24, causing pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 to fail. Desired outcome: permit ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 so echo replies reach the source and reachability is restored.

plan.problem: Inbound access-list R1_IN on router1 Serial0/1/0 contains a deny ICMP for source 172.16.0.0/24, preventing ICMP echo-replies from 172.16.0.11 and causing 0/25 pings to succeed.
plan.fix_summary: Replace the deny ICMP entry in access-list R1_IN with a permit for ICMP sourced from 172.16.0.0/24 (sequence 10) and keep the ACL applied inbound on Serial0/1/0.

parameters: description=Allow ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 for return traffic to restore reachability

parsed.intent_description: R1 is blocking ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24: inbound ACL R1_IN on router1 interface Serial0/1/0 denies ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24, causing pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 to fail. Desired outcome: permit ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 so echo replies reach the source and reachability is restored.

plan.problem: Inbound access-list R1_IN on router1 Serial0/1/0 contains a deny ICMP for source 172.16.0.0/24, preventing ICMP echo-replies from 172.16.0.11 and causing 0/25 pings to succeed.
plan.fix_summary: Replace the deny ICMP entry in access-list R1_IN with a permit for ICMP sourced from 172.16.0.0/24 (sequence 10) and keep the ACL applied inbound on Serial0/1/0.

parameters: description=Allow ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 for return traffic to restore reachability

data.alert_description: R1 is blocking ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24: inbound ACL R1_IN on router1 interface Serial0/1/0 denies ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24, causing pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 to fail. Desired outcome: permit ICMP from 172.16.0.0/24 so echo replies reach the source and reachability is restored.

evidence.detail: R1 ospf: Serial0/1/0 neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.2 (state ospf-nbr-full); R2 ospf: Serial0/1/0 neighbor 1.1.1.1 at 10.0.0.1 (state ospf-nbr-full)
R1 ospf: Serial0/1/1 neighbor 3.3.3.3 at 10.0.0.9 (state ospf-nbr-full); R3 ospf: Serial0/1/0 neighbor 1.1.1.1 at 10.0.0.10 (state ospf-nbr-full)
R2 ospf: Serial0/1/1 neighbor 3.3.3.3 at 10.0.0.6 (state ospf-nbr-full); R3 ospf: Serial0/1/1 neighbor 2.2.2.2 at 10.0.0.5 (state ospf-nbr-full)

messages.content:
What was observed
- Pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 were failing. R1 had an inbound ACL (R1_IN) on Serial0/1/0 that denied ICMP sourced from 172.16.0.0/24.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- An access-list entry on R1 (R1_IN applied inbound on Serial0/1/0) was blocking ICMP replies from the 172.16.0.0/24 network.

Changes made
- Replaced the ACL entry (sequence 10) so ICMP is now permitted (extended ACL R1_IN updated/created with sequence 10 = permit icmp). The ACL remains applied inbound on Serial0/1/0.

Verification
- Passed. Running-config shows R1_IN still applied inbound on Serial0/1/0 and sequence 10 permits ICMP; ACL also contains a permit ip any any fallback. No remaining issues detected.

data.last_message:
What was observed
- Pings from 192.168.0.11 to 172.16.0.11 were failing. R1 had an inbound ACL (R1_IN) on Serial0/1/0 that denied ICMP sourced from 172.16.0.0/24.

Diagnosis (root cause)
- An access-list entry on R1 (R1_IN applied inbound on Serial0/1/0) was blocking ICMP replies from the 172.16.0.0/24 network.

Changes made
- Replaced the ACL entry (sequence 10) so ICMP is now permitted (extended ACL R1_IN updated/created with sequence 10 = permit icmp). The ACL remains applied inbound on Serial0/1/0.

Verification
- Passed. Running-config shows R1_IN still applied inbound on Serial0/1/0 and sequence 10 permits ICMP; ACL also contains a permit ip any any fallback. No remaining issues detected.
